free_text: Photoneutron Fine Structure in 208Pb and Multipole
Resonance
#doi:10.1103/PhysRevLett.35.1215
National Research Council of Canada
linac.
Pulsed bremsstrahlung (6-, 10-, or 20-nsec
duration, 720 Hz) swept clean of electrons and
positrons was produced in a 0.51-mm Ta radiator by
electrons from the NRC linac.
The target was a 795-g disk, 15
cm diam by 4.5 mm thick, containing 91% of 208Pb and
7% of 207Pb.
The neutron detector, a 30-cm-diam by 76-mm-
thick disk of plastic scintillator viewed by four
photomultipliers, was situated 31.2 m from the
photoneutron target at the end of an evacuated flight
tube at 90 to the photon beam.
Conventional time-of-flight techniques
with time resolution of 0.3 to 0.7 nsec/m. A
coincidence between two phototubes constituted an
event. To reduce gamma flash, a 1-cm Bi slab was
placed in front of the detector.
BP
The bremsstrahlung spectrum was measured at each
electron energy but was replaced in the data analysis
by a smooth approximation normalized at 7 MeV. The
ground state differential cross section 208Pb was
constructed using the uppermost 520 keV of each
neutron spectrum.
Fig. 2, page 1217.
An approximate total cross section was obtained by
assuming W(theta) = 2 - P2(cos theta) as the angular
distribution of the Pb photoneutrons.
The total cross section is approximate
since the angu1ar distribution has not been measured.
Fig. 2, page 1217.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
Peaks at about these energies have been
previously observed.
